name: Noise TOP
description: The Noise TOP generates a variety of noise patterns including perlin, simplex, sparse, alligator and random. Some types of noise run on the CPU, while others are calculated on the GPU. The ones that are calculated on the GPU will have GPU in their name.
summary: The Noise TOP generates a variety of noise patterns including perlin, simplex, sparse, alligator and random. Some types of noise run on the CPU, while others are calculated on the GPU. The ones that a

parameters.name: Type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: The noise function used to generate noise.

parameters.name: Perlin 2D (GPU)
parameters.label: Perlin 2D (GPU)
parameters.description: Perlin 2D (GPU) parameter.

parameters.name: Seed
parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description: seed - Any number, integer or non-integer, which starts the random number generator. Each number gives completely different noise patterns, but with similar characteristics.

parameters.name: Period
parameters.label: Period
parameters.description:
period - The approximate separation between peaks of a noise cycle. It is expressed in Units. Increasing the period stretches the noise pattern out.	
Period is the opposite of frequency. If the period is 2 seconds, the base frequency is 0.5 cycles per second, or 0.5Hz for short. Hz refers to Hertz, the electrical and audio engineer of the 19th century, not the car guy.			
If the Type is set to Random, setting this to zero will produce completely random noise. Otherwise, the period should be greater than zero.

parameters.name: Harmonics
parameters.label: Harmonics
parameters.description: harmon - The number of higher frequency components to layer on top of the base frequency. The higher this number, the bumpier the noise will be (as long as roughness is not set to zero). 0 harmonics give the base shape.

parameters.name: Harmonic Spread
parameters.label: Harmonic Spread
parameters.description: spread - The factor by which the frequency of the harmonics are increased. It is normally 2. A spread of 3 and a base frequency of 0.1Hz will produce harmonics at 0.3Hz, 0.9Hz, 2.7Hz, etc.. This parameter is only valid for the Harmonic Summation type.

parameters.name: Harmonic Gain
parameters.label: Harmonic Gain
parameters.description: gain - Amount of the Harmonic Gain layered on top of the base frequency.

parameters.name: Roughness
parameters.label: Roughness
parameters.description:
rough - Controls the effect of the higher frequency noise. When roughness is zero, all harmonics above the base frequency have no effect. At one, all harmonics are equal in amplitude to the base frequency. When roughness is between one and zero, the amplitude of higher harmonics drops off exponentially from the base frequency.	
The default roughness is 0.5. This means the amplitude of the first harmonic is 0.5 of the base frequency, the second is 0.25, the third is 0.125. The harmonics are added to the base to give the final shape. The Harmonics parameter and the Roughness parameter must both be non-zero to see the harmonic effects.

parameters.name: Exponent
parameters.label: Exponent
parameters.description: exp - Pushes the noise values toward 0, or +1 and -1. (It raises the value to the power of the exponent.) Exponents greater than one will pull the channel toward zero, and powers less than one will pull peaks towards +1 and -1. It is used to reshape the channels.

parameters.name: Amplitude
parameters.label: Amplitude
parameters.description: amp - Defines the noise value's amplitude (a scale on the values output).

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description: offset - Defines the midpoint color of the noise pattern, the default is 0.5 grey.

parameters.name: Monochrome
parameters.label: Monochrome
parameters.description: mono - Toggle color or monochrome noise.

parameters.name: Aspect Correct
parameters.label: Aspect Correct
parameters.description: aspectcorrect - Controls if the noise takes aspect ratio into account when calculating it's noise coordinates. When this is off, the noise will stretch to fit a non-square aspect ratio texture.

parameters.name: Transform Order
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description:
xord -  - The menu attached to this parameter allows you to specify the order in which the transforms will take place. Changing the Transform order will change where things go much the same way as going a block and turning east gets you to a different place than turning east and then going a block.

 srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.name: Scale Rotate Translate
parameters.label: Scale Rotate Translate
parameters.description: srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.name: Rotate Order
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description:
rord -  - The rotational matrix presented when you click on this option allows you to set the transform order for the rotations. As with transform order (above), changing the order in which the rotations take place will alter the final position.

 xyz - xzy - yxz - yzx - zxy - zyx -

parameters.name: Rx Ry Rz
parameters.label: Rx Ry Rz
parameters.description: xyz - xzy - yxz - yzx - zxy - zyx -

parameters.name: Translate
parameters.label: Translate
parameters.description:
t -  - Translate the sampling plane through the noise space.

 tx - ty - tz -

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: tx - ty - tz -

parameters.name: Rotate
parameters.label: Rotate
parameters.description:
r -  - Rotate the sampling plane in the noise space.

 rx - ry - rz -

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
s -  - Scale the sampling plane.

 sx - sy - sz -

parameters.name: Pivot
parameters.label: Pivot
parameters.description:
p -  - Control the pivot for the transform of the sampling plane.

 px - py - pz -

parameters.name: Translate 4D
parameters.label: Translate 4D
parameters.description: t4d - When doing 4D noise, this applies a translation to the 4th coordinate. The previous transformation parameters do not affect the 4th coordinate.

parameters.name: Scale 4D
parameters.label: Scale 4D
parameters.description: s4d - When doing 4D noise, this applies a scale to the 4th coordinate.

parameters.name: RGB
parameters.label: RGB
parameters.description:
rgb -  - When an input is connected to the Noise , the noise pattern is placed over the input image using UV coordinates and the settings from this menu.

 noise - Just the noise is output. multiply - The noise is multiplied with the input. add - The input is added to the noise. subtract - The input is subtracted from the noise.

parameters.name: Noise
parameters.label: Noise
parameters.description: noise - Just the noise is output. multiply - The noise is multiplied with the input. add - The input is added to the noise. subtract - The input is subtracted from the noise.

parameters.name: Input Scale
parameters.label: Input Scale
parameters.description: inputscale - Adjusts how much of the input image is added to the output.

parameters.name: Noise Scale
parameters.label: Noise Scale
parameters.description: noisescale - Adjusts how much of the noise is added to the output.

parameters.name: Alpha
parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
alpha -  - Sets the alpha channel for the output image.

 zero - Alpha channel is zero. one - Alpha channel is one. random - Alpha channel is random noise. input - Alpha is the pass through from the input. multiply - Alpha is the result of the input's alpha channel * the noise. add - Alpha is the result of the input's alpha channel + the noise. addclamp - Alpha is the result of the input's alpha channel + the noise but clamped around [0-1].

parameters.name: Zero
parameters.label: Zero
parameters.description: zero - Alpha channel is zero. one - Alpha channel is one. random - Alpha channel is random noise. input - Alpha is the pass through from the input. multiply - Alpha is the result of the input's alpha channel * the noise. add - Alpha is the result of the input's alpha channel + the noise. addclamp - Alpha is the result of the input's alpha channel + the noise but clamped around [0-1].

parameters.name: Dither
parameters.label: Dither
parameters.description: dither - Dithers the output to help deal with banding and other artifacts created by precision limitations of 8-bit displays.

parameters.name: Derivative (Slope)
parameters.label: Derivative (Slope)
parameters.description: derivative - Turn this on to output the derivative (also known as slope) of the noise function. Currently, only supported for Simplex2D, Simplex3D, Perlin2D and Perlin3D noise types. For 2D noise derivatives, the RG channels get populated with the XY derivatives. For 3D noise derivatives, the RGB channels get populated with the XYZ derivatives. Note that this calculates derivative of the noise function not derivatives along the noise image.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Grow or shrink the input resolution to fit this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. limit - Limit the input resolution to be not larger than this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. custom - Directly control the width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Grow or shrink the input resolution to fit this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. limit - Limit the input resolution to be not larger than this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. custom - Directly control the width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail. When the input is 32-bit float format, only nearest filtering will be used (regardless of what is selected).

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. For every pass after the first it takes the result of the previous pass and replaces the node's first input with the result of the previous pass. One exception to this is the GLSL TOP when using compute shaders, where the input will continue to be the connected 's image.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.
